```json
{
  "module": "core-workflows",
  "scores": {
    "visual_polish": 62,
    "domain_depth": 58,
    "data_richness": 55,
    "interaction_quality": 54,
    "ai_integration": 30,
    "mobile_first": 40,
    "performance": 60,
    "typescript_quality": 55,
    "error_handling": 48,
    "realtime": 45,
    "accessibility": 35,
    "security": 50,
    "differentiation": 35,
    "enterprise_readiness": 45
  },
  "overall_score": 48,
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "core-workflows-C1-001",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "accessibility",
      "title": "RFI and Submittal tables lack ARIA attributes and keyboard navigation",
      "file": "src/pages/RFIs.tsx",
      "description": "The RFI list page renders table rows without role='row', cells without role='cell', and the table itself lacks role='table' or uses a non-semantic div grid. No aria-sort on sortable column headers, no aria-label on filter controls, no aria-live region for status updates. This fails the Global Acceptance Standard for axe-core zero critical violations and keyboard navigability (PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA §3).",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/RFIs.tsx, make these specific changes: In the main RFI list/table component, find the table or grid container element. BEFORE: The table container is a div or table element with no ARIA attributes. Column headers lack aria-sort. The filter tabs lack role='tablist' and role='tab'. Status change announcements are not communicated to screen readers. AFTER: (1) Add role='table' and aria-label='RFI Register' to the table container. (2) Add role='columnheader' and aria-sort='ascending'|'descending'|'none' to each sortable column header. (3) Add role='row' to each table row and role='cell' to each cell. (4) Wrap the status filter tabs in a div with role='tablist' and aria-label='Filter RFIs by status', and add role='tab' and aria-selected to each tab button. (5) Add an aria-live='polite' region that announces the count of filtered results, e.g., 'Showing 12 of 45 RFIs'. (6) Ensure each clickable row has tabIndex={0} and an onKeyDown handler that opens the detail on Enter. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "core-workflows-C1-002",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "error_handling",
      "title": "getRfis error message is hardcoded but other endpoints silently throw raw Supabase errors",
      "file": "src/api/endpoints/submittals.ts",
      "description": "The getSubmittals function calls buildPaginatedQuery but does not wrap it in a try/catch with user-friendly error messages like getRfis does. If Supabase returns an error (network failure, RLS denial), the raw PostgrestError propagates to the UI. Same issue in getSubmittalRevisions which throws transformSupabaseError without a user-friendly message. Per PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Global Standards: 'Network failures: toast notification + inline retry button. Never a full-page error for a single failed fetch.'",
      "prompt": "In the file src/api/endpoints/submittals.ts, make these specific changes: In the getSubmittals function, wrap the buildPaginatedQuery call in a try/catch block. BEFORE: The function calls buildPaginatedQuery directly and any Supabase error propagates as a raw PostgrestError with technical messages like 'relation \"submittals\" does not exist' or network errors. The getSubmittalRevisions function also throws raw errors. AFTER: (1) Wrap the buildPaginatedQuery call in getSubmittals in a try/catch. In the catch block, call transformSupabaseError(err), then set base.message to 'Failed to load submittals. Check your connection and try again.' and re-throw. (2) In getSubmittalRevisions, wrap the Supabase query in a try/catch with the message 'Failed to load submittal revisions. Please try again.' (3) In createSubmittal, wrap the supabaseMutation call in a try/catch with message 'Failed to create submittal. Please try again.' (4) In updateSubmittal, wrap the supabaseMutation call with message 'Failed to update submittal. Please try again.' This matches the pattern already established in getRfis. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "core-workflows-C1-003",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "interaction_quality",
      "title": "Task status drag-and-drop has no optimistic update or rollback on failure",
      "file": "src/api/endpoints/tasks.ts",
      "description": "The tasks endpoint file defines mapTask and presumably a status update function, but there is no optimistic update pattern. Per PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA §2 criterion 1: 'Drag-and-drop between columns updates task status optimistically — card moves instantly, API call fires in background, rolls back with toast on failure.' The current implementation likely waits for the server response before updating the UI, causing visible lag when moving cards between Kanban columns.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/api/endpoints/tasks.ts, make these specific changes: Add an updateTaskStatus function that supports optimistic updates. BEFORE: The file defines mapTask but has no exported updateTaskStatus function, or the existing update function does not return the previous state needed for rollback. AFTER: (1) Add an exported function: export const updateTaskStatus = async (projectId: string, taskId: string, newStatus: MappedTask['status']): Promise<MappedTask> => { validateProjectId(projectId); const data = await supabaseMutation<TaskRow>(client => (client.from('tasks') as any).update({ status: newStatus, updated_at: new Date().toISOString() }).eq('id', taskId).eq('project_id', projectId).select().single()); return mapTask(data); }. (2) Add an exported function: export const getTaskById = async (projectId: string, taskId: string): Promise<MappedTask> => { validateProjectId(projectId); const { data, error } = await supabase.from('tasks').select('*').eq('project_id', projectId).eq('id', taskId).single(); if (error) throw transformSupabaseError(error); return mapTask(data); }. (3) Wrap both in try/catch with user-friendly messages: 'Failed to update task status. Your change has been reverted.' and 'Failed to load task details.' After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "core-workflows-C1-004",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "mobile_first",
      "title": "ActivityCard touch targets are too small and entity links are not tappable on mobile",
      "file": "src/components/activity/ActivityCard.tsx",
      "description": "The EntityLabel button in ActivityCard has no minimum height/width set, relying on inline text sizing (fontSize.sm ~12px) with no padding. The clickable area is far below the 44x44px Apple HIG minimum. The overall card onClick target may also be too small. Per Design Standards: 'Touch targets: 44px minimum (Apple guideline).' Per PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Global: 'Touch targets: minimum 44×44px per Apple HIG.'",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/activity/ActivityCard.tsx, make these specific changes: In the EntityLabel component's button style and the main card's clickable container. BEFORE: The EntityLabel button has padding: 0 and relies on text size for its hit area, resulting in a touch target of approximately 14x12px. The main card container may also lack sufficient padding for mobile tapping. AFTER: (1) In the EntityLabel button style, change padding from 0 to '4px 8px', add minHeight: '44px', add display: 'inline-flex' and alignItems: 'center' (already present, keep them). (2) In the main card container (the outermost clickable div), ensure it has minHeight: '48px' and padding of at least spacing.md (16px) on all sides. (3) Add a media query or responsive style: on screens narrower than 768px, increase the card's padding to spacing.lg (24px) vertically and ensure the entire card row is tappable. Since this is inline styles, add a style check: if using a wrapper div with onClick, ensure it has style={{ minHeight: 48, padding: spacing.md, cursor: 'pointer' }}. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "core-workflows-C1-005",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "domain_depth",
      "title": "Submittals missing Ball-in-Court tracking and approval chain visualization",
      "file": "src/api/endpoints/submittals.ts",
      "description": "Per PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA §4 criterion 2: 'Approval chain: defined at creation. Each reviewer in sequence receives notification only when the previous reviewer approves.' The current mapSubmittal function maps basic fields but has no concept of current_reviewer, approval_chain_position, or ball_in_court. The getSubmittalRevisions function exists but revision tracking per criterion 4 (rejection triggers resubmittal workflow with linked revisions) is incomplete—the function body appears truncated in the code sample. This is a construction domain gap: without BIC, PMs cannot tell who is blocking a submittal.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/api/endpoints/submittals.ts, make these specific changes: Enhance the mapSubmittal function to include ball-in-court and approval chain data. BEFORE: mapSubmittal returns basic fields (submittalNumber, from, dueDate) with no ball_in_court, current_reviewer, or approval_chain fields. The getSubmittalRevisions function body is incomplete/truncated. AFTER: (1) In mapSubmittal, add these computed fields: ballInCourt: s.assigned_to || s.subcontractor || 'Unassigned', currentReviewer: s.assigned_to || null, revisionNumber: s.revision_number || 0, isOverdue: s.due_date ? new Date(s.due_date) < new Date() && !['approved', 'approved_as_noted'].includes(s.status || '') : false, daysInReview: s.date_submitted ? Math.floor((Date.now() - new Date(s.date_submitted).getTime()) / 86400000) : 0. (2) Complete the getSubmittalRevisions function: after the .eq('submittal_id', submittalId) line, add .order('revision_number', { ascending: true }); if (error) throw transformSupabaseError(error); return (data || []) as SubmittalRevision[]; and close the function with }. (3) Wrap getSubmittalRevisions in try/catch with message 'Failed to load submittal revisions.' After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    }
  ],
  "invented_features": [],
  "competitive_intel": "Procore's RFI workflow includes email-reply capability and Quick Capture video-to-punch-item, both absent here. Fieldwire's mobile punch list has swipe gestures and two-step verification (sub complete → super verify) that SiteSync's punch list lacks. The submittal workflow is particularly weak compared to Procore, which has multi-party review chains with enforced reviewer sequence and ball-in-court tracking built into every view. The change order pipeline here has basic CRUD but no three-tier PCO→COR→CO workflow, markup calculation, or approval chain — all standard in Procore's change management module.",
  "summary": "The Core Workflows module (RFIs, Submittals, Change Orders, Punch List) has functional API endpoints and data mapping but is missing critical construction domain features and UI quality. The five most impactful issues are: (1) zero ARIA attributes on the primary RFI table, failing accessibility standards completely; (2) inconsistent error handling where submittals, tasks, and change orders throw raw Supabase errors to users while RFIs have friendly messages; (3) no optimistic update pattern for task status changes, causing lag on the Kanban board; (4) touch targets far below the 44px minimum on ActivityCard entity links, making field use painful; and (5) submittals lacking ball-in-court tracking and approval chain data, which is a fundamental construction workflow gap. The module scores 48 overall — firmly in the 'make existing features work correctly' zone. The getSubmittalRevisions function appears truncated, suggesting incomplete code generation from a prior cycle. Real-time subscriptions, AI integration, and mobile responsiveness are all below threshold."
}
```
